harness_type.basis: Harness intent: scenario economics + measured coordinator latency

data_classifications.basis: Allowed data classifications for every emitted datum

node.basis: Node.js runtime version used for this run

platform.basis: OS platform used for this run

arch.basis: CPU architecture used for this run

iterations.basis: Benchmark iteration count selected by operator

sonnet_input_per_1m_usd.basis: Assumed published API list price baseline

input_ratio.basis: Cost normalization assumes 60% input / 40% output tokens

output_ratio.basis: Cost normalization assumes 60% input / 40% output tokens

assumed_usage_window_tokens.basis: User-editable scenario control. Not a verified plan-enforcement fact.

caveat.value: Max-plan window internals are not publicly specified in a way this harness can verify.

native_total_tokens.basis: Scenario total derived from assumed workload inputs

lead_cost_usd_equivalent.basis: Token-equivalent API normalization only; not billing telemetry

lead_minus_native_pct_equivalent.basis: Percent difference between derived token-equivalent values

summary.value: Conflict checks and spawn-block decisions remain sub-millisecond.

description.value: 5 workers, 15 tasks, worker failures, reassignment, handoffs

native_reassignment_tokens_estimate.basis: Scenario estimate for native context reinjection during reassignment

lead_reassignment_tokens.basis: Filesystem reassignment path in Lead does not invoke model tokens

native_reassignment_tokens_estimate.basis: Derived from scenario assumption above; not measured native telemetry

caveat.value: Native reassignment token behavior is modeled, not empirically captured in this harness.

native_alternative.basis: Derived from architecture and feature inspection

evidence.value: Lead coordination (inbox JSONL, task JSON, approval files, heartbeats) is filesystem I/O; measured latency remains sub-millisecond.

claim_classification.basis: Claim rests on locally measured behavior and verifiable implementation

evidence.value: Lead uses appendFileSync O(1)-style append behavior; native sidecar path uses JSON array read-append-write.

claim_classification.basis: Structural property derived from implementation shape

evidence.value: Lead performs pre-spawn file overlap checks; native Agent Teams path does not provide equivalent file-scope conflict blocking.

claim_classification.basis: Feature delta derived from implementation comparison

evidence.value: Lead writes approval files and waits for approval acknowledgements; known native auto-approve issues are tracked separately.

claim_classification.basis: Behavioral claim derived from implementation and tests

verdict.basis: Insufficient local end-to-end telemetry for definitive proof

evidence.value: Lead persists session IDs and supports resume by session id; native resume issues are reported but not fully reproducible in this harness.

claim_classification.basis: Outcome depends on runtime conditions outside this benchmark harness

evidence.value: In the small scenario, lead orchestration overhead can outweigh native coordination overhead.
evidence.basis: Derived from assumed token inputs and token-equivalent math

claim_classification.basis: Economic statement is model-based, not measured billing proof

evidence.value: Medium scenario modeling can show lower coordination pressure for Lead, but this is not native-vs-Lead billing telemetry.

claim_classification.basis: Economic statement is model-based, not measured billing proof

evidence.value: Heavy scenario modeling is directionally favorable for coordination pressure, but still lacks empirical billing and native in-process telemetry.

claim_classification.basis: Economic statement is model-based, not measured billing proof

verdict.basis: Not supportable as a factual claim

evidence.value: Usage-window enforcement details are opaque; this harness cannot prove lower real plan pressure for Lead vs native.

claim_classification.basis: Cannot be elevated beyond speculative status without provider telemetry

evidence.value: Lead exposes conflict detection, governance controls, task board, priority aging, auto-rebalance, handoff snapshots, audit trail, and orchestration controls not present in native Agent Teams preview scope.

claim_classification.basis: Feature-delta claim derived from implementation comparison

evidence.value: This harness does not observe end-to-end billing, so blanket cheaper-than-native claims are unsupported.

value: mcp-coordinator/lib/workers.js — conflict checks and worker governance
code_inspection.basis: Local artifacts used to justify structural claims

value: bench/workflow-benchmark.mjs — scenario model + measured latency harness
benchmark_artifacts.basis: Artifacts used by this benchmark lane

value: Anthropic pricing page (March 2026 baseline) for token-equivalent normalization
Anthropic Max documentation (window enforcement internals remain opaque)
external_references.basis: External context; not directly measured by this harness

claim.value: Lead provides real operator-control features (including conflict detection and governance controls) and uses a filesystem coordination path with 0 API-token overhead on that path. Blanket cheaper-than-native claims remain unsupported without billing telemetry.
claim.basis: Best-supported public claim from measured + inspected evidence

value: Any claim that assumed window percentages reflect provider-enforced reality
